reasoning: The stock dropped sharply from over $200 in early February 2025 to a low of ~$146 in early April due to weak Q4 cloud growth and anxiety over its massive $75B AI capex plans. On April 9, the stock staged a meaningful recovery back to ~$158.85 driven by positive announcements at the Google Cloud Next '25 event. However, Google faces several near-term downdrafts: the high-stakes DOJ antitrust remedies trial starting April 21, and its Q1 earnings expected around April 24. With investors likely to de-risk ahead of the trial and earnings, the very short-term momentum (spanning the April 20 date) remains slightly negative (estimated ~0.45). By May 13, the trial will be concluding and Q1 results will have printed; negative regulatory headlines could still weigh on sentiment, but long-term upward asset drift starts taking effect (extrapolated to 0.47). Over the 3-month (July 12) and 6-month (October 10) horizons, the immediate momentum from Q1's slump will fade and the broader upward market drift for large-cap tech stocks should pull the probability back towards and eventually slightly above the coin-flip baseline (0.49 and 0.51, respectively), satisfying the increasing horizon uncertainty.
